title: National Media Platforms and Mobile Transformation — Mediacorp, Singapore
summary: National-scale media, election, news, streaming, CMS, mobile, Apple Watch, and corporate experience architecture across Mediacorp.
body:
National media platform and mobile-transformation work at Mediacorp spans news, elections, streaming, CMS ecosystems, mobile apps, Apple Watch, and corporate UX guidelines in Singapore.

Included projects
- Singapore National Elections
- Channel NewsAsia web CMS
- Channel NewsAsia iOS and Android apps
- Apple Watch app UX for Channel NewsAsia
- Toggle/mewatch VoD
- Toggle/mewatch CMS
- ELLE Singapore CMS
- Corporate-wide UX Guidelines
- Corporate site CMS
- Channel 8 News CMS
- Today Online News web CMS
- Today Online News apps CMS

Strategic and technical themes
- National-scale broadcasting and digital-platform architecture.
- CMS ecosystem design across editorial, streaming, corporate, and news products.
- Mobile-first product transformation across apps and connected-device surfaces.
- Experience standards and UX guidelines that improve coherence across many products and teams.

Proof connection
- This programme may reference the existing Mediacorp 30× monthly-active-audience proof where relevant.
- It must not imply that every listed project independently generated that growth.

Retrieval guidance
- Use this record for Mediacorp, Channel NewsAsia, elections, Apple Watch, VoD, CMS, or corporate UX-guidelines questions.

claims: Joz worked across national-scale media, news, streaming, mobile, and CMS platforms at Mediacorp.
The programme included Channel NewsAsia, elections-related work, Apple Watch UX, and corporate UX guidelines.
proof_points: The broader Mediacorp proof already covers 30× monthly active audience growth and mobile-first transformation.
This grouped programme record exists to answer employer-specific questions with more concrete project detail.
projects: Apple Watch app UX for Channel NewsAsia
Channel NewsAsia iOS and Android apps
metadata.source_notes: Grouped programme record. The 30× MAU proof belongs to the broader Mediacorp transformation context, not each individual sub-project.
